ai.headline: AI chatbots linked to at least a dozen wrongful death/harm lawsuits; ~1 in 6 adults using AI for mental health advice
ai.body: A recent KFF poll found ~16% of US adults have used AI tools for mental health information or advice, with higher rates among younger and uninsured adults. OpenAI faces at least a dozen lawsuits alleging wrongful death or serious harm after ChatGPT users were hospitalised or died by suicide. The gap between AI chatbot availability and clinical safety guardrails is becoming a material liability surface for AI platform operators.

mkt.headline: Strait of Hormuz closure (9 weeks): second-order economic shocks spreading beyond energy into fertilisers, food, and supply chains
mkt.body: Oil at ~$120/barrel is now cascading into fertiliser shortages, higher farm input costs, and tighter food producer margins — beginning to show in Q1 2026 earnings. Energy and fertiliser players are outperforming while downstream food, retail, and hospitality face margin compression. Structural accelerants include faster EV adoption and supply-chain re-routing investment.

mkt.headline: Google Cloud growth reshapes AI race; Big Tech combined capex tops $700 billion
mkt.body: Google Cloud results are cited as a standout in reshaping the competitive AI infrastructure race, with aggregate Big Tech AI spending crossing $700B. This is directly relevant to GOOGL, MSFT, and AMZN capex trajectories and cloud revenue mix. Continued spend at this scale supports NVDA and TSMC demand visibility.

mkt.headline: TSMC exits Arm investment with $231M share sale
mkt.body: TSMC has divested its stake in Arm Holdings for $231M, signalling a portfolio rationalisation rather than a strategic shift in the semiconductor ecosystem. For TSMC holders, it removes a modest cross-holding but has no impact on core foundry operations or AI chip demand. Huawei separately forecasts a 60% surge in AI chip revenue, reinforcing non-US AI silicon competition.

mkt.headline: Atlassian jumps 25% after-hours on earnings beat; Paramount nears Warner Bros. Discovery acquisition after shareholder approval
mkt.body: Atlassian's outsized after-hours move suggests enterprise software spend remains resilient despite macro headwinds. Paramount-WBD deal progression, if completed, would create a major consolidated streaming/media entity and further concentrate the advertising market relevant to Alphabet's competitive landscape.

deepDives.title: Strait of Hormuz second-order economic impact: full earnings and industry analysis
deepDives.why: Oil at $120 with fertiliser and food chain contagion is a macro input directly relevant to inflation expectations, Fed/rate trajectory, and portfolio sector positioning across AMZN (retail cost pressure) and VOO/VEA (broad market exposure).
